description:
A junior network-engineer has copied three raw troubleshooting logs into the
    directory  /home/user/debug_logs.  
    You have been asked to package them, test an extraction, and write a short
    verification report so that someone else can quickly confirm the archive’s
    integrity.

    1.  Compress exactly the three files shown below into a single gzip-compressed
        tar archive named  net_debug_backup.tar.gz  and place the archive in
        /home/user/archive /   (create the directory first if it does not exist).

           /home/user/debug_logs/ping.log  
           /home/user/debug_logs/traceroute.log  
           /home/user/debug_logs/interface_status.log  

    2.  Extract that archive into a brand-new directory called
        /home/user/restored_logs .

    3.  Inside  /home/user/restored_logs  create a text file named
        verification.txt  whose *exact* contents are **exactly four CSV-style
        lines**, in this precise order and spelling (newline after every line):

            FileName,LineCount
            ping.log,7
            traceroute.log,4
            interface_status.log,4

        •  The header must be spelled **FileName,LineCount**  
        •  Use **commas**, no spaces, and **seven / four / four** as the line
           counts exactly as shown above.  
        •  There must be a trailing newline after the last line.

    4.  When everything is finished the directory tree should look like this:

            /home/user/archive/net_debug_backup.tar.gz
            /home/user/restored_logs/verification.txt
            /home/user/restored_logs/ping.log
            /home/user/restored_logs/traceroute.log
            /home/user/restored_logs/interface_status.log
            /home/user/debug_logs/(original three logs still present)

    The automated checker will confirm:

        •  The archive exists at the exact path and contains *only*
           ping.log, traceroute.log, interface_status.log.  
        •  The three files were successfully extracted into
           /home/user/restored_logs  and are byte-for-byte identical to the
           originals.  
        •  verification.txt exists at the specified path and its contents match
           the four-line format above *exactly*, including the newline at the
           end of the fourth line.

    Do not display or remove any of the log contents in your answer; just work in
    the shell so the files and report meet the specifications.
truth:
Initial file system state BEFORE the agent begins:

    1. Directory /home/user/debug_logs/ (0755) containing three UTF-8 text files:

       a. /home/user/debug_logs/ping.log (0644)
          Contents (7 lines, each ending with LF):
            PING google.com (142.250.72.46) 56(84) bytes of data.
            64 bytes from lga34s34-in-f14.1e100.net (142.250.72.46): icmp_seq=1 ttl=115 time=12.3 ms
            64 bytes from lga34s34-in-f14.1e100.net (142.250.72.46): icmp_seq=2 ttl=115 time=12.7 ms
            
            --- google.com ping statistics ---
            2 packets transmitted, 2 received, 0% packet loss, time 1001ms
            rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 12.302/12.509/12.716/0.207 ms
          (There is a blank physical line between third and fifth lines.)

       b. /home/user/debug_logs/traceroute.log (0644)
          Contents (4 lines, LF line endings):
            traceroute to google.com (142.250.72.46), 30 hops max, 60 byte packets
             1  192.168.1.1 (192.168.1.1)  2.123 ms  2.112 ms  2.099 ms
             2  10.10.0.1 (10.10.0.1)  10.415 ms  10.394 ms  10.374 ms
             3  142.250.72.46 (142.250.72.46)  15.467 ms  15.451 ms  15.432 ms

       c. /home/user/debug_logs/interface_status.log (0644)
          Contents (4 lines):
            eth0: flags=4163<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,MULTICAST>  mtu 1500
                    inet 192.168.1.100  netmask 255.255.255.0  broadcast 192.168.1.255
                    RX packets 1024  bytes 123456 (120.5 KiB)
                    TX packets 1000  bytes 110000 (107.4 KiB)

    2. No other /home/user/archive or /home/user/restored_logs directories exist.

    Expected FINAL state after correct completion:

    • /home/user/archive/  (0755) exists and contains a gzip-compressed tar
      file /home/user/archive/net_debug_backup.tar.gz whose tar member names are
      exactly:
          ping.log
          traceroute.log
          interface_status.log

    • /home/user/restored_logs/ (0755) exists and contains:
          ping.log               (exact byte-for-byte copy of original)
          traceroute.log         (exact copy)
          interface_status.log   (exact copy)
          verification.txt       (0644) with 4 lines:
              FileName,LineCount\n
              ping.log,7\n
              traceroute.log,4\n
              interface_status.log,4\n

    • The originals in /home/user/debug_logs remain unchanged.

    The automated test will:
        1. Use tar -tzf to inspect archive members.
        2. Compare md5sum of each restored file against the original.
        3. Check verification.txt SHA256 against the string:
              "FileName,LineCount\nping.log,7\ntraceroute.log,4\ninterface_status.log,4\n"
name: task_000000_3d339274
